_comment: Single source of truth for model presets, consumed by BOTH the bash CLI
(olivia.sh, via presets.py) and the Python model router (model_router.py).
Previously this data lived in olivia.sh's preset_field()/normalize_preset()
case statements; it was extracted here so the router and the CLI agree on
exactly which preset maps to which model / container / node.
aliases            - alternate names the CLI and router accept (case-insensitive)
model              - HuggingFace repo id passed to vLLM as MODEL (the served name unless overridden)
served_model_name  - optional; what vLLM serves the model AS (defaults to `model`). Routing also matches this.
nodes / gpus / pp  - SLURM/vLLM shape: NUM_NODES, GPUs-per-node (intra-node TP), pipeline-parallel size
index              - default container index for the preset
port               - vLLM serve port. Defaults to 8000 for every preset because each server job owns
its GPU node(s) exclusively, so two models never collide on a node — the router
tells them apart by the per-preset SLURM job name (vllm-server-<preset>), not the
port. Override per preset only if you ever co-locate servers on one node.

glm51_v19.description: GLM-5.1 744B MoE+DSA, AWQ-4bit, vLLM v0.19.0 (recipe default)

glm51_v20.description: GLM-5.1 on vLLM v0.20.0 + RayExecutorV2 (quarantined; same wedge)

kimi.description: Kimi K2.6 1T MoE (32B active), native int4, MLA, multimodal

kimi27.description: Kimi K2.7-Code 1T MoE (32B active), native int4 (reuses kimi container)

laguna.description: Laguna M.1 225B MoE (23B active), block-FP8, single-node TP=4, CUDAGraph on
